Students can identify and write numbers 1 to 5.
Students can count objects up to 10 accurately.
Students can distinguish between big and small objects.
Students can identify objects at the top or bottom.
Students can describe relative closeness of objects.
Students can order events or numbers sequentially.
Students can categorize items into one or many.
Students can combine two groups of objects.
Students can remove objects from a group and count remainder.
Students can count up to 10 and group objects in tens.
Students can identify 1, 2, 5, and 10 rupee coins and notes.
Students can measure length using handspans or feet.
Students can name the days of the week in sequence.
Students can solve addition problems with carry-over.
Students can solve subtraction problems with borrowing.
Students can name and classify basic geometric shapes.
Students can identify and extend simple patterns.
Students can identify months and find dates on a calendar.
Students can identify heavier and lighter objects.
Students can read and interpret simple pictographs.
Students can represent groups as repeated addition.
Students can distribute objects equally among groups.
Students can pronounce basic letter sounds.
Students can use polite words in conversation.
Students can identify nouns in simple sentences.
Students can recognize verbs in daily activities.
Students can match rhyming words in poems.
Students can replace naming words with correct pronouns.
Students can use basic prepositions correctly.
Students can read common words like the, of, to, and.
Students can read short sentences independently.
Students can classify items as living or non-living.
Students can label parts of a plant.
Students can identify what different animals eat.
Students can list sources of fresh water.
Students can demonstrate air properties through simple experiments.
Students can practice healthy hygiene habits.
Students can describe ways to protect the environment.
Students can decompose a two-digit number into tens and ones.
